# Fix Plan (cycle 3)
**Generated by plan-runner aggregator on 2026-05-18**
**Source bugs report:** docs/plan-runner/2026-05-18/cycle-2/bugs.md
**Original plan:** docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-18-dnd-weapons-phase-5-acquisition.md

These three Phase 5 source files now fail `:{version}:compileKotlin` on every MC
version (1.20.1, 1.21.1, 1.21.4, 1.21.11, 26.1.2). The cycle 1 Stonecutter parse
error has been resolved; this cycle's compile failures were previously masked.

After all three tasks are complete, the verification matrix and
`phase-5-verification-final.md` must be re-run (see Task 4).

---

## Task 1: Fix WitherTrophyHandler.kt -- ServerLivingEntityEvents unresolved on all 5 versions
**File:** src/main/kotlin/com/dndweapons/loot/WitherTrophyHandler.kt
**From bugs:** wave-2-agent-1-bug-1 (severity P0, category missing_requirement)

### Acceptance criteria
- `./gradlew chiseledBuild` advances past `:{version}:compileKotlin` for this file on all 5 MC versions.
- Stonecutter-guarded imports resolve `ServerLivingEntityEvents` on every version (Fabric API module path differs across MC versions).
- The lambda body's references to `entity.type`, `entity.spawnAtLocation(...)`, `entity.random.nextInt(...)` type-check on every version (`spawnAtLocation` was renamed in newer versions).
- No behavior change -- this is an API-binding fix only.
- Commit with message `phase 5 fix: WitherTrophyHandler per-version Fabric event API`

### Context
Compile errors (sample from one version):
```
WitherTrophyHandler.kt:6:51 Unresolved reference 'ServerLivingEntityEvents'.
WitherTrophyHandler.kt:21:9  Unresolved reference 'ServerLivingEntityEvents'.
WitherTrophyHandler.kt:21:57 Cannot infer type for value parameter 'entity'.
WitherTrophyHandler.kt:22:24 Unresolved reference 'type'.
WitherTrophyHandler.kt:28:48 Function invocation 'random()' expected.
WitherTrophyHandler.kt:28:55 Unresolved reference 'nextInt'.
WitherTrophyHandler.kt:29:20 Unresolved reference 'spawnAtLocation'.
```

Same pattern on every version (1.20.1 / 1.21.1 / 1.21.4 / 1.21.11 / 26.1.2). The
unresolved root is `ServerLivingEntityEvents`; once its import is resolved per-version
the lambda parameter `entity` will type-check and the subsequent member accesses
will follow. Use the same stonecutter guard idiom already used in
`WeaponLootRegistrar.kt` for `LootTableEvents` (v2 vs v3 module paths around 26.1.2).

---

## Task 2: Fix AcquisitionGametest.kt -- vanilla loot/item API drift unresolved on all 5 versions
**File:** src/main/kotlin/com/dndweapons/test/AcquisitionGametest.kt
**From bugs:** wave-2-agent-1-bug-2 (severity P0, category missing_requirement)

### Acceptance criteria
- `./gradlew chiseledBuild` advances past `:{version}:compileKotlin` for this file on all 5 MC versions.
- `./gradlew chiseledRunGametest` discovers and runs the @GameTest methods in this class on all 5 versions.
- Stonecutter-guarded accessors resolve the following on each version:
  - `MinecraftServer.lootData` vs `getServer().reloadableRegistries().lookupOrThrow(Registries.LOOT_TABLE)` (or equivalent per-version)
  - `LootTable.getRandomItems` vs `LootTable.getRandomLootItems` (rename across versions)
  - `Item.descriptionId` direct field vs `Item.getDescriptionId()` accessor
  - `ResourceLocation.parse` vs `ResourceLocation.tryParse` vs `new ResourceLocation(String)` ctor
- Commit with message `phase 5 fix: AcquisitionGametest per-version vanilla API`

### Context
Compile errors (samples across versions):
```
AcquisitionGametest.kt:60:17 Cannot infer type for type parameter 'R'.
AcquisitionGametest.kt:62:31 Unresolved reference 'lootData'.
AcquisitionGametest.kt:63:65 Unresolved reference 'parse'.        (1.20.1)
AcquisitionGametest.kt:79:27 Unresolved reference 'getRandomItems'. (1.21.4+)
AcquisitionGametest.kt:80:28 Unresolved reference 'descriptionId'.
AcquisitionGametest.kt:117:24 Unresolved reference 'descriptionId'.
AcquisitionGametest.kt:142:24 Unresolved reference 'descriptionId'.
```

The `parse` failure on 1.20.1 confirms `ResourceLocation.parse` does not exist there
(use ctor instead). The 1.21.4+ `getRandomItems` failure suggests the method was
renamed mid-version-range.

---

## Task 3: Fix WeaponTradeRegistrar.kt -- VillagerTrades.TRADES API drift unresolved on all 5 versions
**File:** src/main/kotlin/com/dndweapons/trade/WeaponTradeRegistrar.kt
**From bugs:** wave-2-agent-1-bug-3 (severity P0, category missing_requirement)

### Acceptance criteria
- `./gradlew chiseledBuild` advances past `:{version}:compileKotlin` for this file on all 5 MC versions.
- The trade registration at line 67-69 compiles against the version's actual `VillagerTrades.TRADES` type signature.
- Resolves `VillagerTrades.TRADES.get(profession).add(level, factories)` or the appropriate per-version equivalent (raw map vs registry-holder map vs Int2ObjectMap<Int2ObjectMap<List<ItemListing>>>).
- Resolves the `profession` -> `VillagerProfession!` argument-type mismatch (currently inferred as `Any`).
- Commit with message `phase 5 fix: WeaponTradeRegistrar per-version villager trade API`

### Context
Compile errors:
```
WeaponTradeRegistrar.kt:67:34 None of the following candidates is applicable:
WeaponTradeRegistrar.kt:67:57 Argument type mismatch: actual type is 'Any', but 'VillagerProfession!' was expected.
WeaponTradeRegistrar.kt:67:78 Cannot infer type for value parameter 'factories'.
WeaponTradeRegistrar.kt:69:35 Unresolved reference 'add'.
```

Two viable resolution paths:
1. **Per-version stonecutter forks of the registration block** -- mirror the four-way
   version split already used elsewhere in the codebase.
2. **Use Fabric API's `TradeOfferHelper.registerVillagerOffers(...)`** where available --
   abstracts over the vanilla map drift but may not exist on 1.20.1.

Prefer option (2) on versions that support it, fall back to (1) on 1.20.1.

---

## Task 4: Re-run verification matrix and update phase-5-verification-final.md
**File:** docs/superpowers/plans/phase-5-verification-final.md
**Depends on:** Tasks 1, 2, 3 (must be in a later wave -- do not run until all three compile fixes are in)

### Acceptance criteria
- Run `./gradlew chiseledBuild` on all 5 MC versions and confirm BUILD SUCCESSFUL for each.
- Run `./gradlew chiseledTest` on all 5 MC versions and confirm all JUnit tests pass, including the 7 AcquisitionCatalogTest tests.
- Run `./gradlew chiseledRunGametest` on all 5 MC versions and confirm 16/16 gametests pass per version.
- Update the verification matrix in phase-5-verification-final.md with actual results (GREEN or FAIL per cell).
- If all 5 versions are GREEN across all 3 columns: set Status to GREEN, apply git tag `phase-5-acquisition`, and push.
- If any version is RED: set Status to HOLD, leave tag unapplied, document the failure in the Known Issues section.
- Commit with message `phase 5: final verification doc (cycle 3)`

### Context
Cycle 2's matrix was all-FAIL (compile errors). Cycle 1's matrix was all-FAIL
(Stonecutter parse). Two prior cycles of blockers must be cleared before this task
can possibly succeed.
